title: `--runtime=` flag
body:
# **kind feature**

**Description**

`podman run -it --runtime=kata fedora bash`
where the path and custom flags for that runtime are configured in /etc/...

**Describe the results you received:**



**Describe the results you expected:**

Ability to easily use runtimes other than runc

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.10.1.3
Go Version:    go1.10.4
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: this would likely need the config for array of `runtime_path` to be more like a map, than an array

I've been thinking about this one for a while - I'd consider it a blocker for CRI-O integration.

We need to modify `libpod` to allow multiple OCI runtimes, and then modify containers to ensure that they store the runtime they were created with in the database (and modify the database to ensure that all Podman processes using it have the same runtimes defined - we get into an awkward spot if Podman A creates a Kata container, and Podman B tries to start that container but doesn't have the Kata runtime available).

It probably also makes sense to add checks that all containers in a pod must use the same runtime, and all dependencies of a container must share the same runtime (we can't expect namespace sharing on the host to work right when some containers are launched in VMs).
(coincidentally kata-runtime works nicely currently by setting `runtime_path=["/usr/bin/kata-runtime"]` in libpod.conf)
I believe @giuseppe Also got gvisor to work with podman.
railcar, not so much. even if you set cgroupfs as the cgroup manager (https://github.com/oracle/railcar/issues/41) there is something else failing
just did a first step in a branch. Currently will just use the first item in the `runtimes=` set, or runc if nothing is configured. https://github.com/vbatts/containers-libpod/tree/multiple_runtimes

We should try and maintain backwards compatibility if possible - can we
retain the old runtime field and use it first if present (maybe give it the
name "default") and then allow new runtimes to be set in addition?

I also like that we allowed an array of paths before, since distros have a
way of putting things in different paths.

On Fri, Nov 2, 2018, 10:30 Vincent Batts <notifications@github.com wrote:

> like:
>
> [[runtimes]]
>   Name = "runc"
>   Paths = ["/usr/bin/runc"]
>
> [[runtimes]]
>   Name = "kata"
>   Paths = ["/usr/bin/kata-runtime"
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1750#issuecomment-435398399>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCJIJFC0loWH44_os_sY0A_pqMXK8ks5urFbwgaJpZM4YLj2z>
> .
>
I'd also add an `Options` item to complement `{Name, Paths}`
@marcov i figured additional flags could come later

> We should try and maintain backwards compatibility if possible - can we retain the old runtime field and use it first if present (maybe give it the name "default") and then allow new runtimes to be set in addition?

could we use `exec.LookPath` instead of writing this map in the configuration file?  It won't be possible to assign an arbitrary name to the runtime, but it will keep compatibility with what we have now.

Alternatively, I think that if an absolute path is used, we should still use it without lookup.  I find it very useful to test different runtimes that are installed in temporary paths
@vbatts @giuseppe Where are we with this issue?

@rhatdan since I did the PoC branch https://github.com/vbatts/containers-libpod/tree/multiple_runtimes I have not been able to circle back on this, but feel it is very needed for wider adoption and use of all the runtimes. Anyone is welcome to pick up my branch.
